project_summary: Consume the Aitri v2.0.0-rc.161 status --json additions (top-level lastSession, per-pipeline quality_gates/ac_coverage) and retire the two workarounds that existed only because those fields were missing: the collector's direct .aitri.local read (the acknowledged SCHEMA.md deviation) and the FR-047 technical_debt from contract-catchup-rc159. North Star KPI: zero SCHEMA.md deviations in the collector for rc.161+ projects. JTBD: when the dashboard shows a project, its session marker and code-quality gate results come from the sanctioned contract surface, so QA can trust them without the Hub touching per-machine files. Guardrail metric: records for pre-rc.161 projects remain byte-identical (additive-only).

functional_requirements.title: Prefer snapshot lastSession over the .aitri.local inline read
functional_requirements.description: When the aitri status --json payload contains the lastSession key (rc.161+), projectFromSnapshot carries it into the record and the collector performs NO direct .aitri.local read for that project. The inline read survives only as a fallback for payloads that lack the key (pre-rc.161 CLIs). A snapshot lastSession of null is trusted as null (no session yet) — it does not trigger the fallback, because the key's presence proves the CLI speaks the rc.161 contract.
acceptance_criteria: Given a snapshot with lastSession: {at, agent, event}, the record's lastSession equals that object's event/agent/at and fs.readFileSync of .aitri.local is never called for that project
Given a snapshot WITHOUT the lastSession key, the existing inline .aitri.local fallback runs exactly as before and populates the record
Given a snapshot with lastSession: null, the record's lastSession is null and the fallback does NOT run

functional_requirements.title: Project per-pipeline quality_gates into the record
functional_requirements.description: projectFromSnapshot carries tests.perPipeline[].quality_gates ([{name, status, required, threshold?, measured?}] | null) into the project record, additive and absent-tolerant: entries with a non-null array produce a record field preserving name, status (pass|fail|error|null), required flag and the optional threshold/measured numbers; null or absent adds no key. null is never interpreted as an Aitri version signal (the artifact fields predate rc.161 — only the projection is new).
acceptance_criteria: Given a snapshot whose root perPipeline entry has quality_gates [{name:'lint',status:'fail',required:true}], the record preserves name, status and required verbatim
Given quality_gates: null on every perPipeline entry, the record carries no quality_gates key
A coverage-style gate {name:'coverage',status:'pass',required:true,threshold:80,measured:92} keeps threshold and measured in the record

functional_requirements.title: Project per-pipeline ac_coverage into the record unchanged
functional_requirements.description: projectFromSnapshot carries tests.perPipeline[].ac_coverage (array of {ac_id, fr_id, tests_passing, tests_failing, tests_skipped, tests_manual, status} | null) into the project record as an unchanged pass-through, additive and absent-tolerant: null or absent adds no key.
acceptance_criteria: Given ac_coverage [{ac_id:'AC-001',fr_id:'FR-001',tests_passing:2,tests_failing:0,tests_skipped:0,tests_manual:0,status:'covered'}] on the root entry, the record contains the identical array
Given ac_coverage: null, the record carries no ac_coverage key

functional_requirements.title: Integration manifest reviewed baseline advances to 2.0.0-rc.161
functional_requirements.description: After the rc.160 + rc.161 integration CHANGELOG entries are reviewed (both marked — additive), ~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json reviewedUpTo is set to 2.0.0-rc.161 so the version-gap integration alert stops flagging rc.160/rc.161 projects. Data change performed once at ship time; the manifest reader itself is untouched.
acceptance_criteria: evaluateIntegrationAlert raises no alert for a project on aitriVersion 2.0.0-rc.161 once the manifest is updated
The manifest file remains valid JSON and keeps its existing fields

functional_requirements.title: Snapshot projection unit coverage for the new fields
functional_requirements.description: The projectFromSnapshot test suite gains cases for the three new surfaces (lastSession preference + both quality surfaces), including the absent-tolerant negatives, replacing the skipped TC-047h/e/f placeholders from contract-catchup-rc159 with running tests.
acceptance_criteria: npm test runs the new projection cases with zero skipped among them
The contract-catchup-rc159 technical_debt note for FR-047 is closed by reference to this feature in its record

user_personas.goal: Read session recency and code-quality gate verdicts from the dashboard record and trust their provenance
user_personas.pain_point: Gate results never reach the record (FR-047 debt), and session data comes from a per-machine file the contract tells consumers not to read

user_personas.goal: Keep the collector on sanctioned contract surfaces only, so Aitri Core upgrades never silently break ingestion
user_personas.pain_point: The .aitri.local inline read is a documented deviation that must be carried and re-justified on every schema review

user_stories.i_want: the collector to take lastSession from the status --json payload when present
user_stories.so_that: the SCHEMA.md deviation disappears for current-CLI projects

user_stories.i_want: quality gate names and verdicts stored in the project record
user_stories.so_that: future QA views can render lint/type-check/security results without another collector change

user_stories.i_want: AC-level coverage stored in the record
user_stories.so_that: acceptance-criterion traceability is available to the workspace

user_stories.i_want: the reviewed-contract baseline to advance to rc.161
user_stories.so_that: the integration alert only fires for genuinely unreviewed versions

user_stories.i_want: running tests for the new projection paths
user_stories.so_that: the FR-047 debt closes with evidence instead of moving

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Snapshot-path records for projects on pre-rc.161 CLIs stay identical: lastSession still populated via the inline fallback, and no quality_gates/ac_coverage keys appear
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Existing snapshot-reader tests for payloads without the new keys pass unchanged

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The legacy/fallback reader path (aitri-reader.js, snapshot-ineligible projects) is untouched — same reads, same record shape
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: aitri-reader test suite passes with zero modifications

non_functional_requirements.requirement: bugs.parseErrors consumption (rc.158) and the version-gap integration alert keep working unchanged
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Existing parseErrors + evaluateIntegrationAlert tests pass with zero modifications

non_functional_requirements.requirement: No new file reads outside the project directory and the existing manifest path; the change strictly REMOVES a per-machine file read for rc.161+ projects
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Code review confirms no new fs access paths; the .aitri.local read is reachable only on the fallback branch

no_go_zone: No new UI sections — rendering quality_gates/ac_coverage in QA Workspace tabs is a separate feature
No coverage-audit freshness consumption (Core deliberately did not ship it; the Traceability-tab re-hash stays)
No changes to detail-reader.js on-demand artifact reads
No validate --json consumption changes (Deploy Verdict already reads top-level deployable)
constraints: Additive-only on the record schema: existing dashboard.json consumers must keep working without reading the new fields

idea_provenance_sources.problem: This repo's own recorded debt: lib/collector/index.js lastSession inline-read comment ('Core feedback filed to expose lastSession…retire this inline read') + FR-047 technical_debt in features/contract-catchup-rc159/spec/04_BUILD_REPORT.json
idea_provenance_sources.users: Existing Hub personas (QA auditor, maintainer) from contract-catchup-rc159 — no new user types
idea_provenance_sources.baseline: Aitri Core v2.0.0-rc.161 shipped 2026-07-07 (docs/integrations/CHANGELOG.md, — additive) exposing exactly these fields
idea_provenance_sources.no_go_zone: FEATURE_IDEA.md Out of Scope — mirrors the FR-047 boundary drawn in rc159

coverage_map.need: stop reading .aitri.local when the contract provides lastSession
quality gate results reach the project record (FR-047 debt)
AC-level coverage reaches the project record (FR-047 debt)
integration alert stops flagging reviewed rc.160/rc.161
render the new surfaces in QA Workspace tabs

original_brief:
<!-- Ground truth: user-directed 2026-07-07 ("dale a todo" — consume the rc.161
     contract). Problem/Why sourced from this repo's own recorded debt, not
     inferred: index.js lastSession inline-read comment + FR-047 technical_debt
     in features/contract-catchup-rc159/spec/04_BUILD_REPORT.json. -->

## Feature
Consume the Aitri v2.0.0-rc.161 `status --json` additions (`lastSession`, per-pipeline `quality_gates`/`ac_coverage`) and retire the two workarounds that existed only because those fields were missing.

## Problem / Why
Core shipped rc.161 today with the surfaces this Hub filed as HUB-CATCHUP-0705. Until the Hub consumes them: (a) the collector keeps reading `.aitri.local` directly on the snapshot path — the acknowledged SCHEMA.md deviation recorded in lib/collector/index.js; (b) FR-047 of contract-catchup-rc159 stays open as technical_debt ("blocked on Core exposing quality_gates/ac_coverage"), so QA-facing views cannot ever render gate results from the record.

## Target Users
Existing Hub users (QA/PM personas reading dashboard.json-backed views). No new user types.

## New Behavior
The system must...
- Prefer the snapshot's top-level `lastSession` when the key is present in `aitri status --json` output, without falling back to `.aitri.local` for that project.
- Keep the `.aitri.local` inline fallback ONLY when the snapshot payload lacks the `lastSession` key (pre-rc.161 CLI).
- Carry `tests.perPipeline[].quality_gates` (name/status/required + optional threshold/measured) into the project record when present, additive and absent-tolerant.
- Carry `tests.perPipeline[].ac_coverage` into the project record unchanged when present, additive and absent-tolerant.
- Add no field to the record when the snapshot lacks these surfaces (`null` or absent → no key), and never interpret `null` as "old Aitri version" (the artifact fields predate rc.161; only the projection is new).

## Success Criteria
- Given a snapshot payload with `lastSession: {at, agent, event}`, when the collector projects it, then the record's lastSession matches the snapshot and no `.aitri.local` read occurs for that project.
- Given a snapshot payload without the `lastSession` key (older CLI), when the collector runs, then the existing inline fallback still populates lastSession as before.
- Given a snapshot whose root perPipeline entry carries `quality_gates` with a failed required gate, when projected, then the record preserves gate name, status and required flag.
- Given a snapshot whose perPipeline entries have `quality_gates: null` and `ac_coverage: null`, when projected, then the record carries no quality_gates/ac_coverage keys.
- The three TC-047h/e/f test cases skipped in contract-catchup-rc159 (or their equivalents here) run and pass.
- After review, `~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json` `reviewedUpTo` = `2.0.0-rc.161`.

## Touch Points
- MODIFIES `lib/collector/snapshot-reader.js` (`projectFromSnapshot`) — new field projection.
- MODIFIES `lib/collector/index.js` — lastSession preference order (snapshot key wins; inline `.aitri.local` read becomes the pre-rc.161 fallback only).
- MODIFIES `~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json` (data, not code) — reviewedUpTo bump after review.
- Existing FRs: completes the intent of FR-047 (contract-catchup-rc159, shipped as technical_debt); touches FR-016 (lastSession rendering) data source only.

## Must Not Break (Regression Boundary)
- Snapshot-path collection for projects on pre-rc.161 CLIs keeps producing the same record (lastSession via inline fallback; no quality_gates/ac_coverage keys).
- The legacy/fallback reader path (`aitri-reader.js`, snapshot-ineligible projects) remains byte-identical in behavior.
- QA Workspace detail tabs (detail-reader.js) keep reading artifacts directly — no change to on-demand reads.
- The `bugs.parseErrors` consumption (rc.158) and the version-gap integration alert keep working unchanged.
- dashboard.json records for projects WITHOUT the new fields keep their exact current shape (additive-only guarantee).

## Out of Scope
- No new UI sections — rendering quality_gates/ac_coverage in QA Workspace tabs is a separate feature (same boundary FR-047 drew).
- No coverage-audit freshness consumption (Core deliberately did not ship it; the Traceability tab re-hash stays, deferred until a real project mis-reports).
- No `validate --json` changes (rc.160's `allValid` fix is corrective upstream; the Deploy Verdict card already reads top-level `deployable`).
